description_long: This is a rounded ceramic teapot with a bulbous body, a gently curved spout, and a large loop handle. The exterior is adorned with a striking geometric pattern of concentric blue and white circles interspersed with vertical teal leaf motifs, all set against a white background. The spout and handle are both decorated with the same blue and white circular motif, creating a cohesive and decorative appearance. The teapot features a matching lid with a small, rounded knob, also patterned in blue and white. The surface appears glossy and smooth, typical of glazed ceramics. This teapot is both functional and decorative, likely to stand out in any setting due to its vibrant, traditional Polish pottery-inspired design.
description: A rounded, glossy ceramic teapot with blue, white, and teal geometric patterns, featuring a curved spout, loop handle, and matching patterned lid.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the teapot, highlighting the curved spout on the left and the large loop handle on the right, with the decorative pattern clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front view focusing on the spout, with the circular motif running along its length and the lid's knob centered above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the spout on the right and the handle on the left, mirroring the features of view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the handle prominently, with the patterned body and lid visible, and the spout mostly hidden from view.
attribute_spout_orientation: Curved spout angled slightly upward, positioned at the front of the teapot body.
attribute_handle_design: Large, rounded loop handle attached at the upper and lower sides of the body, moderately thick and ergonomically shaped.
attribute_lid_presence: Present; domed lid with a small rounded knob, fits snugly on top.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Geometric concentric circles in blue and white with vertical teal leaf motifs, covering the body, spout, handle, and lid.
